assessment.label: Proof offered — review pending
assessment.summary: A source presents a full proof, but no qualifying full-scope check has passed.

credited_to.note: Keep the verified double-root statement distinct from the stronger uniqueness/exclusion claim.

limitations: Keep the verified double-root statement distinct from the stronger uniqueness/exclusion claim.
evidence.scope: The PGL2 action on the projective dual of binary cubic forms.
evidence.summary: Tao states the orbit classification and explicitly analyzes the double-root case.

limitations: The stronger assertion that only the double-root orbit can yield affine three-space is not included: the source does not give a full invariant exclusion of the other two cases.
notes: Tao reports that he did not identify a known computable invariant forcing the special slice to be affine, while explicitly inviting a more authoritative algebraic-geometric answer. This is a qualified research assessment, not a nonexistence theorem. [Source](https://terrytao.wordpress.com/2026/07/21/a-digestion-of-the-jacobian-conjecture-counterexample/comment-page-1/#comment-693529)

scope: The PGL2 action on the projective dual of binary cubic forms.

sources.title: A digestion of the Jacobian conjecture counterexample

statement: Under the projective linear action, dual binary-cubic hyperplanes have three orbits, indexed by the root partitions (3), (2,1), and (1,1,1).
title: Three symmetry orbits of dual binary-cubic hyperplanes
